parameterGroups.label: Event Data
parameterGroups.rationale: Both parameters define which event data slot to read and what value to use when the slot is empty

parameters.name: SlotIndex
parameters.description: The event data slot index to read from (0-15)

parameters.name: DefaultValue
parameters.description: The value to use when the event data slot hasn't been written

gaps.question: How are event data slots written? Is it via the GlobalRoutingManager scripting API, via Message.setEventData(), or some other mechanism? What writes to these slots before the modulator reads them?
gaps.context: Users cannot use this modulator without knowing how to write data into slots - the read side is useless without the write side

gaps.question: The description says '0-15' but the range max is 16.0. Is slot 16 valid, or is the max value exclusive? Are there really 16 or 17 available slots?
gaps.context: Off-by-one confusion could lead to silent failures if users set an out-of-range slot

gaps.question: As a VoiceStartModulator, this reads the slot once at note-on. Does the value remain constant for the entire voice lifetime? If the slot is written after note-on, does the voice see the updated value?
gaps.context: VoiceStartModulator semantics imply sample-and-hold at note-on, but users may expect live updates

gaps.question: How does the modulator detect that a slot 'hasn't been written'? Is there a sentinel value, or does it check a written flag? What happens if the slot is written with 0.0 - does it use 0.0 or the DefaultValue?
gaps.context: The distinction between 'not written' and 'written with zero' is important for correct usage

gaps.question: Is event data per-note (attached to HiseEvent), per-channel, or global? Can different notes have different values in the same slot?
gaps.context: The scope determines whether this is truly per-voice or effectively monophonic

notes: Very simple VoiceStartModulator with only 2 parameters. No interfaces, no mod chains, no table processing. The module is a consumer of the event data system - the producer side (how data gets written) is the main knowledge gap. The description mentions 'global routing manager' which suggests the GlobalRoutingManager scripting API is involved. SlotIndex range discrepancy (description says 0-15, range goes to 16) needs verification. Natural seeAlso candidates: EventDataEnvelope (continuous version), GlobalRoutingManager (write side).
